content:
# Joshua Chapter 22 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 246-247 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_022_page_001.jpg`

1. Then Joshua summoned the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh,

2. and said to them: "You have kept all that Moses the servant of YHWH commanded you, and you have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you.

3. You have not forsaken your brothers these many days, to this day, and you have kept the charge of the commandment of YHWH your God.

4. And now YHWH your God has given rest to your brothers, as he spoke to them. Now therefore turn and go to your tents, to the land of your possession, which Moses the servant of YHWH gave to you on the other side of the Jordan.

5. Only be very careful to observe the commandment and the Torah that Moses the servant of YHWH commanded you: to love YHWH your God, and to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments, and to cling to him, and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul."

6. And Joshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their tents.

7. Now to the one half of the tribe of Manasseh, Moses had given a possession in Bashan, and to the other half, Joshua gave a possession among their brothers on this side of the Jordan, to the west. And also when Joshua sent them away to their tents, he blessed them.

8. And he spoke to them, saying: "Return to your tents with great wealth, and with very much livestock, with silver, and with gold, and with bronze, and with iron, and with very many garments. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers."

9. And the sons of Reuben, and the sons of Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh returned and departed from the Sons of Israel at Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan, to go to the land of Gilead, to the land of their possession, which they had taken possession of according to the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

10. And they came to the region of the Jordan that is in the land of Canaan. And the sons of Reuben, and the sons of Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh built there an altar by the Jordan — an altar great in appearance.

11. And the Sons of Israel heard it said: "Behold, the sons of Reuben, and the sons of Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh have built an altar opposite the land of Canaan, in the region of the Jordan, on the side belonging to the Sons of Israel."

12. And when the Sons of Israel heard this, the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh to go up against them for war.

13. And the Sons of Israel sent to the sons of Reuben, and to the sons of Gad, and to the half-tribe of Manasseh, into the land of Gilead, Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest,

14. and ten chiefs with him — one chief for each father's house, for each of the tribes of Israel — and each one of them was the head of a father's house among the thousands of Israel.

15. And they came to the sons of Reuben, and to the sons of Gad, and to the half-tribe of Manasseh, to the land of Gilead, and they spoke with them, saying:

16. "Thus says the whole congregation of YHWH: 'What is this treachery that you have committed against the God of Israel, to turn away this day from following YHWH, by building for yourselves an altar, to rebel this day against YHWH?

17. Is the iniquity of Peor too little for us, from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day, and for which there was a plague upon the congregation of YHWH,

18. that you must turn away this day from following YHWH? And it shall be, if you rebel today against YHWH, then tomorrow he will be angry with the whole congregation of Israel.

19. But indeed, if the land of your possession is unclean, cross over to the land of the possession of YHWH, where the tabernacle of YHWH stands, and take possession among us. But do not rebel against YHWH, and do not rebel against us, by building for yourselves an altar besides the altar of YHWH our God.

20. Did not Achan the son of Zerah commit a trespass concerning the devoted things, and wrath fell upon the whole congregation of Israel? And that man did not perish alone in his iniquity.'"

21. Then the sons of Reuben, and the sons of Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh answered and spoke to the heads of the thousands of Israel:

22. "The God of gods, YHWH! The God of gods, YHWH! He knows! And Israel, it shall know! If it was in rebellion or if in treachery against YHWH — do not save us this day —

23. if we have built for ourselves an altar to turn away from following YHWH, or if to offer upon it burnt offering or grain offering, or if to make upon it peace offerings, let YHWH himself require it.

24. But truly, out of concern for a matter we have done this, saying: 'In time to come your sons might say to our sons, "What have you to do with YHWH, the God of Israel?

25. For YHWH has set the Jordan as a boundary between us and you — you sons of Reuben and sons of Gad — you have no portion in YHWH." So your sons might cause our sons to cease fearing YHWH.'

26. Therefore we said: 'Let us now act to build for ourselves the altar — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice —

27. but it shall be a witness between us and you, and between our generations after us, that we may perform the service of YHWH before him with our burnt offerings, and with our sacrifices, and with our peace offerings, so that your sons may not say to our sons in time to come, "You have no portion in YHWH."'

28. And we said: 'If they should say this to us or to our generations in time to come, then we will say: "Look at the replica of the altar of YHWH, which our fathers made — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice — but it is a witness between us and you."'

29. Far be it from us that we should rebel against YHWH and turn away this day from following YHWH, by building an altar for burnt offering, for grain offering, or for sacrifice, besides the altar of YHWH our God that stands before his tabernacle."

30. And when Phinehas the priest, and the chiefs of the congregation — the heads of the thousands of Israel who were with him — heard the words that the sons of Reuben, and the sons of Gad, and the sons of Manasseh spoke, it was good in their eyes.

31. And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest said to the sons of Reuben, and to the sons of Gad, and to the sons of Manasseh: "Today we know that YHWH is in our midst, because you have not committed this treachery against YHWH. Now you have delivered the Sons of Israel from the hand of YHWH."

32. And Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest, and the chiefs, returned from the sons of Reuben, and from the sons of Gad, from the land of Gilead, to the land of Canaan, to the Sons of Israel, and brought back word to them.

33. And the matter was good in the eyes of the Sons of Israel. And the Sons of Israel blessed God, and they did not speak of going up against them for war, to destroy the land in which the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad were dwelling.

34. And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad called the altar — for "it is a witness between us that YHWH is God."

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH (יהוה):** Rendered consistently as "YHWH" throughout, preserving the Tetragrammaton as it appears in the Leningrad Codex rather than substituting "the LORD" or "Adonai." This is a core LOGOS Bible convention.

2. **"Sons of Israel" (בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל):** Rendered literally throughout per LOGOS convention rather than the interpretive "Israelites." Distinguished from "sons of Reuben" / "sons of Gad" etc. (with lowercase "sons") when referring to specific tribal subgroups rather than the whole covenantal people.

3. **"The God of gods, YHWH!" (אֵל אֱלֹהִים יהוה, v. 22):** The Hebrew uses three divine terms in superlative construction: *El*, *Elohim*, and the Tetragrammaton. This emphatic oath formula appears also in Deuteronomy 10:17 and Psalm 136:2. Rendered literally to preserve the solemn, oath-like force of the Hebrew repetition. The phrase is repeated for emphasis in the MT.

4. **"treachery" (מַעַל, ma'al):** This term carries the specific sense of covenantal unfaithfulness or sacrilege — a breach of trust against YHWH. Used in vv. 16, 20, 22, 31. Consistently rendered "treachery" to distinguish it from general rebellion (*marad*, מָרַד).

5. **"the iniquity of Peor" (עֲוֹן פְּעוֹר, v. 17):** Reference to the apostasy at Baal-Peor (Numbers 25:1-9) where Israel joined in worship of Moabite gods and a plague struck the congregation. Phinehas — the same priest now leading this delegation — was the one who stopped that plague by his zeal (Num 25:7-8), giving his words here particular authority.

6. **"replica" (תַּבְנִית, tavnit, v. 28):** Literally "pattern," "form," or "model." The same word is used for the pattern of the tabernacle shown to Moses on the mountain (Exodus 25:9). The eastern tribes emphasize that their altar is a copy or representation — not a rival cultic site.

7. **"the tabernacle of YHWH" (מִשְׁכַּן יהוה, vv. 19, 29):** The Hebrew *mishkan* ("dwelling place") is rendered "tabernacle" throughout, consistent with LOGOS convention for this technical cultic term. The tabernacle at Shiloh represents YHWH's sole authorized place of sacrifice, which is the crux of the dispute.

8. **Achan / Zerah (v. 20):** The Leningrad Codex reads עָכָן (*Akhan*), rendered "Achan" per standard English convention. He is identified as the son of Zerah (cf. Joshua 7:1 where the fuller genealogy is given through Carmi). The rhetorical appeal to Achan's sin underscores the theology of corporate responsibility — one man's trespass brought wrath on the whole congregation.

9. **"called the altar" (v. 34):** The MT lacks an explicit name for the altar; the Hebrew reads וַיִּקְרְאוּ ... לַמִּזְבֵּחַ followed by the explanatory כִּי clause. The dash before "for" preserves the abrupt syntax of the Hebrew, where the naming is implied rather than stated. Some manuscripts and versions supply "Ed" (עֵד, "witness") as the name.

10. **"on the other side of the Jordan" (בְּעֵבֶר הַיַּרְדֵּן, vv. 4, 7):** The Hebrew *ever* means "the crossing" or "the other side." The perspective shifts depending on the speaker's vantage point — from west looking east in vv. 4, 7 (Canaan perspective), reflecting the narrator's position west of the Jordan.

11. **"fathers' house" (בֵּית אָב, v. 14):** A kinship unit larger than a household but smaller than a clan (*mishpahah*). Rendered literally to preserve the patrilineal social structure reflected in the Hebrew text.

12. **Capitalization — "Sons of Israel" vs. "sons of Reuben/Gad":** Uppercase "Sons" is reserved for the full covenantal designation בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל when it refers to the nation as a whole. Tribal subgroups ("sons of Reuben," "sons of Gad," "sons of Manasseh") use lowercase, as these are literal patronymic descriptions rather than the national title.
